summary: Use gross dynamic limit for pegged orders for matching engine
description_text: Background In Nucleus Matching, pegged orders use a dynamic limit calculated off a reference CP+ level. With fees and discounts, we need to be explicit about whether this dynamic limit is net or gross and how the fee is applied. Issues The calculated dynamic limit pegged to CP+ should be interpreted as a  net  level, not a gross level, as was done in NUMA-62. The fee in this context is the  net fee  (with any discounts already applied). Changes For Match Requests, calculate dynamic limit for pegged orders, and then derive the gross limit from this net limit by backing out the applicable net fee (including discounts). For this, reuse or extend existing utilities for dynamic limit and fee calculations. Flags This functionality is backwards compatible (i.e. without an existing fee schedule the behavior will be the same as it is currently), so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

Issues We need to change the CP+ drop and the API to send relevant fields only and more closely resemble the order market data update drop which minimizes the business logic on X-Pro side. Changes We need to change the CP+ API to only include the following fields: instrumentId cpPlusBid cpPlusOffer cpPlusMid Only instrumentId should be a required field. CP+ drop should also be modified to reflect these changes. Flags This is a non-production functionality so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Modify guardrails validator to use gross limit price for comparison
description_text: Background There is a validator which ensures that the limit price (or dynamic limit calculated from pegged order details) is within the bounds of the static guardrails for the session. Issues The validation is done against the submitted net limit level instead of the gross level. Changes Update the validator to use the gross level instead of net level. Flags Non-breaking as this how the functionality works prior to the introduction of fees. Areas is code.

summary: Mid-X One-Off Items
description_text: Summary REQUIRED – Provide a short summary of the Epic, at most one or two sentences. Goals REQUIRED – What are the goals of this proposal? Multiple goals are best presented in list form. Non-Goals Describe any goals you wish to identify specifically as being out of scope for this proposal. Success Metrics Describe the criteria for closing this Epic successfully. If the success of this work can be gauged by specific numerical metrics and associated goals then describe them here. Motivation Why should this work be done? What are its benefits? Who's asking for it? How does it compare to the competition, if any? Description REQUIRED – Describe the enhancement in detail: Both what it is and, to the extent understood, how you intend to implement it. Summarize, at a high level, all of the interfaces you expect to modify or extend, including external interfaces (FIX, drops, feeds), applications, and Java APIs. Explain how failures in applications using this enhancement will be diagnosed, both during development and in production. Describe any open design issues.

summary: Add total interest to the order based market data drop
description_text: Background There is an order-based market data drop (i.e. market data per order). Issues The total interest, which is a new fields on the AuctionsMarketData event, is not being populated on the drop orders. Changes Add the total interest field. Update any tests to ensure that it is being set correctly. Update any relevant documentation. Flags Non-breaking as the field is optional. Areas is code, external.

summary: Acceptance Tests for Maximum Orders Constraints
description_text: Background The matching engine enforces maximum orders constraints to control how many active orders can exist for user or session. These constraints help prevent runaway order creation and protect system stability. Issues There are currently no Beaker acceptance tests that verify how maximum orders constraints behave end-to-end. Changes Create a suite of Beaker acceptance tests to verify that maximum orders constraints are applied correctly. Flags This work only adds automated tests and does not change production functionality, so it is non‑breaking. Areas is code.

summary: Adhere to Maximum Orders and Maximum Order Per Trader Session Settings
description_text: Background There are configured maximums for orders in a Matching session and orders from a single trader in a Matching session. Issues The maximums are not being applied to live sessions. Changes Add two validations in the Matching Validation Bot which enforce these session-level maximum values. An incoming order does not put the total number of orders above the configured limit of maximum orders per session. An incoming order does not put the total number of orders from a given user/trader above the maximum orders per user per session. Add integration tests to cover these scenarios. Flags Changes are non-breaking as, while a current consumer of the Matching API would not have been limited in the number of orders they can possibly successfully submit to a session, the consumer can still submit as many orders as they wish with the possibility of receiving an OrderReject response instead of an OrderAccept response. Areas is code.

summary: Add Maximum Orders and Maximum Orders Per Trader to Session Settings
description_text: Background There are AuctionsSettings and MidxSettings definitions in matching. Issues These settings need to support a configurable maximum of total orders per session as well as total orders per session per user. Changes Add two optional long fields to these settings (maxOrders and maxOrdersPerTrader). Additionally update the API documentation to include the new fields. Flags Changes are non-breaking as no configuration for new fields will be treated as no maximum. Areas is code, system, external.

summary: Matching Maximum Number of Orders Limits
description_text: Summary Currently Auctions (and Mid-X) has no ability to limit the number of orders accepted by a session. This is an important constraint to implement due to the potential for performance issues that may arise due to unexpected load on the system (e.g. someone decides to upload a million orders into an Excel template). Goals To implement the functionality described in   . In summary, to reject any flex order request which would put the number of total orders for a given session over the number of maximum allowable orders per session, or would put the number of orders within a given session which are attributable to a given user over the maximum allowable number of orders per user per session. Non-Goals It is a non-goal to respond to any intra-day updates to the configured maximums for matching sessions. Success Metrics The success of this epic would require the aforementioned functionality to be implemented, including any relevant Beaker integration and acceptance tests, integrated with the Data Bridge utility property feed and verified to be working as expected in XPro/QA. Motivation The primary motivation of this epic is to mitigate the potential risk of performance slowdowns due to any unexpectedly large number of orders submitted in a given session or submitted by any individual user in a given session. Description The following changes will be required: Changes to the API documentation to add new fields for both Auctions and Mid-X settings Adding these fields to commands/mutators Adding additional validation on order-entry to adhere to the fields' constraints on a per-session and per-user-per-session basis Integration and acceptance testing for both settings definitions and order-entry validation scenarios.

summary: Add VenueId and SessionId Writers for MatchingFixToFlexOrderRequestCommandWriter
description_text: Background When processing FIX NewOrderSingle messages, the MatchingFixToFlexOrderRequestCommandWriter must set the required venueId and sessionId fields to ensure proper order routing and execution. Issues Currently, venueId and sessionId are populated with the incorrect or default values. Changes Implement VenueIdCommandWriter, use VenueService and the FIX MKTXWorkflowType field to derive and write the correct venueId to the FlexOrderRequest command. Implement SessionIdCommandWriter, use the FIX TimeInForce field to determine the session name.   Map AT_OPEN to "MA Opening Auction" and AT_CLOSE to "MA Closing Auction".  Use AuctionsSessionService  to look up the session by name and write the correct sessionId to the FlexOrderRequest command. Update beaker tests to validate that venueId and sessionId are correctly written when processing inbound FIX  NewOrderSingle messages for Auctions orders. Flags Areas: external (updates FIX gateway), code Type: non-breaking (new feature, not yet in production) Hence feat/code.

summary: Consume fees schedule and discount data from Data Bridge topic
description_text: Background REQUIRED – Provide a fellow developer the necessary background to understand the Story. Issues REQUIRED – Describe the problems with the current state of the world given in the previous section. Changes REQUIRED – Describe what changes are necessary to address the issues in the previous section. Flags REQUIRED – Provide the areas for the story (internal, code, system, external) and says whether the changes are breaking or non-breaking. Explains why for the Areas and the breaking value.

summary: Acceptance testing for Auctions fees support
description_text: Background As part of introducing fee support in Auctions, we changed the logic to use gross/net levels when needed which relies on correct fee calculations. However, existing Beaker acceptance tests for Auctions do not cover fee scenarios or the new gross/net level behavior, and there is currently no automated verification that the new fee logic works end-to-end across all supported venues and order types. Issues We need automated Beaker test coverage to: Validate that Auctions correctly handles fee schedules (including discounts) when determining net and gross levels. Ensure that match requests sent to the Exchange bot reflect the expected gross levels derived from user-entered net levels. Cover both limit and pegged orders in Auctions fee scenarios. Verify that Auctions fee behavior is consistent when integrated with X-Pro and Bondlink flows. Provide a reliable safety net for future changes to Auctions fee logic. Changes Design and implement new Beaker acceptance tests that exercise Auctions fee support across relevant scenarios (different fee schedules, discounts, order types and venues). Extend or refactor existing Beaker test fixtures and utilities as needed to support fee-related inputs, calculations, and assertions. Add specific Beaker tests to validate integration paths involving X-Pro and Bondlink, ensuring fees and derived gross/net levels behave as expected end-to-end. Flags This work is test-only and non-functional for production behavior, so it’s non-breaking, and areas is code.

summary: Use gross levels to determine the matching level in Exchange bot
description_text: Background Auctions does not currently support fees, so the levels that used for matching in the Exchange bot are the same levels that are entered by the users, or in case of pegged orders they are equal to the calculated limit levels based on the reference CP+ levels. Issues With the introduction of fees in Auctions, the requirement is to use gross levels for matching engine in the Exchange bot (gross level is the level that does not include the fee). Users will be entering a net level (a level that already includes the fee) as their limit levels for limit orders, so we need to back out the fee from it before sending the match request to the Exchange bot. For pegged orders, the limit level is calculated based on the reference CP+ value, that level is already a gross level so we don’t need to back out the fee from it. The fee will be the net fee (with any discounts already applied). Changes Add methods to calculate gross level from net, net level from gross and get the fee for an order to the FeeHelper utility class, including support for fee discount Use FeeHelper in AuctionsMatchRequestPriceFieldWriter to calculate gross level for the order’s limit price and set it on the MatchRequest command Note: Beaker tests for this will be added as part of   , since this involves making many changes to the existing Beaker tests. Flags This is a new functionality that is backwards compatible (if fee schedules are not present, the behavior is the same as before), so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Apply fee discount in net level calculation

Issues REQUIRED – Describe the problems with the current state of the world given in the previous section. summary: Send fees and net levels on the Fill request drop
description_text: Background There is a fill request drop which drop relevant data for a trade fill to Bondlink. Issues Fee data, which is required in Bondlink to enable charging fees at all, is missing in the drop. Changes Add the following fields on the Fill Request Drop: fillLevel - this is the gross level (level with no fee included) netLevel - this is the level with the fee included (this is the one to use as trade level) fee - this is the fee from the schedule (basically fee with no discount) feeDiscount - this is the discount netFee - this is the fee adjusted by the fee discount Flags Non-breaking as these are new optional fields. Areas is code, external.

summary: Send the net level on the Auctions Order Market Data drop
description_text: Background There is a drop for per-order auctions market data. Issues Since users are submitting a net level (includes fees) for both limit and pegged orders, the net level should be included in the market data drop for the order. Changes Update the auctions order market data drop fields and mutator to include the net clearing level. Additionally, since priceType and marketSegment are part of both MatchingOrderFields and MatchingFlexOrderFields, remove them from both and add them to the MatchingFlexOrderCommonFields. Flags Non-breaking as this is a new optional field. Areas is code, external.

summary: Update Matching API docs to include fees
description_text: Background We have an existing API documentation for Auctions that does not contain fee related fields. Issues With the introduction of fees in Auctions, we need to update the API documentation to include fee related fields, such as the fee, net level, discount, etc. Changes Update the API documentation for Matching to include fees in the following places: Fill request - include fee and net level Order market data - include net matching level Fee schedule and company/venue/product settings - add Auctions venue Flags This is documentation only ticket, so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Auctions Fees Support
description_text: Summary This epic is about supporting Auctions transaction fees. Goals To implement support for transaction fees in Auctions, requirements described here: Non-Goals It’s a non-goal to support estimated prices for spread/yield orders (prices calculated from a net spread/yield) Success Metrics Success of this epic would mean that the Auctions fees support functionality has been implemented, tested in Beaker acceptance tests, integrated with X-Pro and Bondlink and successfully verified in QA. Motivation To allow the ability to charge transaction fees in Auctions and therefore increase revenue. Description The following is needed to implement this functionality. Consume the fees schedule and discount data from the topic provided by data bridge Apply fee/discount to the user-entered or calculated (for pegged orders) level to determine the gross limit level Use the gross limit levels for determining the gross matching level Publish net dynamic limit level for pegged orders on the order market data drop Send both net and gross fill levels along with the net and gross fee and discount to Bondlink in the fill request message

description_text: Background When processing FIX orders, the  MatchingFixToFlexOrderRequestCommandWriter  must set the required  marketSegment  field to ensure proper order routing and execution. Issues Currently,  marketSegment  is not being set, which can cause: Incorrect order routing Failed order processing Changes Implement  FixedIncomeRuleCommandWriter  to determine market segment Update related unit tests Add integration beaker test for validation Flags Areas: external (updates FIX gateway), code Type: non-breaking (new feature, not yet in production) Hence feat/code.

description_text: Background Refer to Issues A patch is needed that applies the work done in    to the maintenance release. Changes Apply the work in    to the maintenance branch. Flags This is a bug fix patch so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Multiple venue CLOSED messages cause session workflow bot to go into infinite loop
description_text: Background When venue status = CLOSED is received, Cancel Bot is triggering session termination request for any live sessions with that venue. Issues As we have seen QA, multiple venue status = CLOSED messages coming in at once cause the session workflow manager to go into infinite loop and eventually run out of memory. The VM log shows the following: 2026-01-26T17:58:00.429000-05:00 qa2ashnuc01 INFO qad-vm-matchingqa3-005.1[1168318] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot ReusableActionRequest [ACTIONREQUEST3] Action completed unsuccessfully: reason=The operation failed due to a changed state, additionalInfo=Pending session status is NONE: expectedPendingSessionStatus=PENDING_TERMINATE 2026-01-26T17:58:00.430000-05:00 qa2ashnuc01 WARNING qad-vm-matchingqa3-005.1[1168318] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot AcceptRejectAuctionsSessionTerminateRequestWorkflow [WORKFLOWSTATE1] An action request completed unsuccessfully due to unexpected state: id=85728 2026-01-26T17:58:00.437000-05:00 qa2ashnuc01 INFO qad-vm-matchingqa3-005.1[1168318] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot ReusableActionRequest [ACTIONREQUEST3] Action completed unsuccessfully: reason=The operation failed due to a changed state, additionalInfo=Pending session status is NONE: expectedPendingSessionStatus=PENDING_TERMINATE 2026-01-26T17:58:00.437000-05:00 qa2ashnuc01 WARNING qad-vm-matchingqa3-005.1[1168318] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot AcceptRejectAuctionsSessionTerminateRequestWorkflow [WORKFLOWSTATE1] An action request completed unsuccessfully due to unexpected state: id=85728 Changes TERMINATE_REQUEST_RESPONSE pending action is not being cleared properly, causing this issue. Flags This is a bug fix, so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Parent/Child Consistency Checks for Pegged Order Fields
description_text: Background Pegged orders have fields pegType and pegOffset, which are sent on both the parent order (flex) and child order requests. Issues There are no consistency checks for these fields. Changes Implement the consistency checks for these fields in Auctions. Flags Non-breaking as this is new functionality. Areas is code.

summary: Publish total interest data at order entry
description_text: Background In   we added total interest qty support for the Auctions market data drop. Issues However, currently total interest is published starting at book building phase, along with other imbalance data. The requirement is to publish total interest only starting at the session’s order entry phase. Changes Make a change in AuctionsExchangeBot to send market data command if totalInterest is positive even if there is no clearing level (currently if there is no clearing level the command is not sent) Schedule indicative match requests at order entry time instead of book building In the  MutableAuctionsMarketDataImbalanceFieldsHelper , check the session phase and if it’s OrderEntry, set total interest only on the drop record Also, refactor the market data drop to use a new business object and new message group. It’s better to decouple it from the current AuctionsMarketData which is used by the session service for order based market data drop. Flags This is new functionality that is non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Create a CP+ Drop
description_text: Background Nucleus receives CP+ data on a feed. Issues There is no drop for CP+ data. Changes Create a drop for CP+ data. This change includes all of the following: Create a Business Object representation of a CP+ record with the following fields: InstrumentId Side Price Type Level (for each of Price, Spread, Yield, DM, 4 total fields) Mid (for each of Price, Spread, Yield, DM, 4 total fields) Create a service to track the business objects (message group) Create an API to describe the fields included in the drop Create a Mutator and Helper which will listen to CompositePlus events and update the Business Objects accordingly Create a drop application (.commands) Wire the drop in beaker and include a set of basic integration tests Flags Non-breaking as this is new functionality. Areas is code, systems, external.

summary: Auctions One-Off Items
description_text: Summary This epic is for Auctions related BAU one off items - bugs, small enhancements, etc. Goals The goal is to track all Auctions one-off items in this epic. These items should not fall into a bigger epic. Non-Goals None Success Metrics This epic should stay open indefinitely. Motivation We want to group one-off items that don’t fall into bigger epics in this epic, to make it easier to classify items. As a rule, each Jira item (story, bug, task, etc) should be under some epic. This is the epic for those items that cannot be classified under any other epics. Description These are one off items, such as bugs, patches, small enhancements, etc. The items are standalone and should be clearly defined in their own tickets.

summary: DW Hedge value on OLU should be rounded to the nearest 1000
description_text: Background In   we put in a rounded logic for the DW hedge value. Issues However, it’s rounded to the nearest whole number, which still makes it a decimal value on X-Pro UI, since it divides the qty by 1000. So if OLU has: "durationWeightedHedgeQty":334788 Then X-Pro displayes 334.788 as dw hedge qty. Changes DW Hedge qty needs to be rounded to the nearest 1000, not the nearest whole number. Flags Non-breaking. Areas is code.

summary: Add a MessageDefinitionValidator for workflow order request writer to validate limit price based on price type
description_text: Background In  CODE-6292  we removed EmptyOrPositiveLongValidator from limit price field writer in OrderRequestOrderStipulationsFieldsComposer.compose. Issues With the above change, limit price is no longer validated in workflow order request writers. It's ok for now since limit price is validated in the feed and is copied from the order into MatchingOrderRequestDefinition used by the writer. However, we do need a separate validator for limit price that takes into account order's price type. If priceType is anything but PRICE, limit price is allowed to be non-positive. Changes Create a MessageDefinitionValidator that takes MatchingOrderRequestDefinition and does the validation described above. Add this validator to MatchingOrderRequestMessageWriter Add Breaker end to end acceptance tests to test that negative spreads/yields/DMs are accepted and match Flags This is a non-breaking change and areas is code.

summary: Write Total Interest Qty to AuctionsMarketData command
description_text: Background Current market data drop includes the following fields: clearingLevel, imbalanceQty, matchedQty and imbalanceSide. Issues We need to add another field called TotalInterestQty, which is the sum of all order quantities per instrument book. More info and requirements here:  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/browse/MG-10 . Before this field can be added to the drop, we need to add it to the AuctionsMarketData command and populate it on both indicative and final matches. Offsetting orders are not counted in total interest qty for indicative matches, but counted for the final match if they are included in the matched qty calculation. Changes Add totalInterestQty field to AuctionsMarketData entity/command Add totalInterestQty field to AuctionsMatchingBook In AuctionMatcher, calculate totalInterest and add it to the book In AuctionsExchangeMarketDataHelper, set the totalInterestQty from the book on the command Flags This is a new functionality so it's non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Modify DelegatingMarketDataMutator and helpers to include missing imbalance fields
description_text: Background There is a mutator for the AuctionsMarketData business object which listens to AuctionsMarketDataEvents. Issues The business object does not contain all the relevant fields for the imbalance. The following fields are missing: imbalanceQty imbalanceSide matchLevel matchQty Changes Extend the existing business object and mutator to include the imbalance fields. Write a new helper for the mutator. Flags Non-breaking as this is new functionality. Areas is code.

Issues There are no acceptance tests for the drop. Changes Create a suite of acceptance tests for the drop. summary: Enforce Maximum Qty Settings in Auctions MarketData Drop Helper
description_text: Background There is a drop with AuctionsDropMarketData as the business object. Issues The AuctionsDropMarketData object contains a number of quantity fields, but does not adhere to the maximum qty rules. Changes Use the FixedIncomeDropMaxQtyService in the MutableAuctionsMarketDataImbalanceFieldsHelper to enforce the maximum qty on the imbalanceQty, matchedQty and totalInterest fields. Additionally, add maximum values for total interest in the service (HG - 20,000,000, HY - 10,000,000). Flags Non-breaking as this does not change functionality and adheres to the api. Areas is code.

summary: Create Delegating Market Data Drop application
description_text: Background There is an existing AuctionsMarketDataDrop based on the AuctionsMarketDataService. Issues The drop is not configured as an application yet. Changes After the addition of required fields in [CODE-6313] Modify DelegatingMarketDataMutator and helpers to include missing imbalance fields - Jira v2.0 Create an application with this drop using the new mutator and business object type. Additionally, swap out the various sessionGuid/orderGuid builders for a static util method in MatchingUtils. Flags Non-breaking as this is new functionality, areas is code.

summary: Auctions Market Data Instrument Drop
description_text: Summary Currently, market data (imbalance qty, matched qty, etc) is being published to X-Pro on the order level. If user does not have any orders, they won't be able to see any market data. We need to publish market data to X-Pro on the instrument level. That is, market data should be published for any eligible instrument that has orders in the session. Goals The goal is to create Market Data drop for an instrument. Non-Goals Removing the current order based market data drop is not required, Success Metrics This epic should be closed after there is a successful test with X-Pro that demonstrates that all the Auctions market data information is available for eligible instruments in the UI as expected. Motivation Users should be able to see market data even if they don't have orders in a session. Description API The first step is to create the API for sending the per instrument market data information to X-Pro. The API is the JSON schema for the messages. The API messages needs to cover at least the following fields. Indicative Match Level Indicative Match Quantity Indicative Imbalance Side Indicative Imbalance Quantity Total Interest (new field, described here:  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/browse/MG-10 ) In addition the following two fields are required to be distributed post match. Final Match Level Final Match Quantity Total interest should be published every 15 seconds starting at the order entry phase. The rest of the fields should be published every 15 seconds starting at book-building phase. In addition, CP+ values for instruments should be published every 15 seconds as well. Services and Mutators This new drop will depend on the existing AuctionsMarketDataService. There is already a mutator for the AuctionsMarketData business objects, as well as AuctionsMarketDataEvent that is dispatched whenever market data changes. We also need to create a different drop for CP+ data. Drop and Beaker tests We need to create the drop app and beaker tests for the created …

summary: Add Back Order Mass Cancel Request And Replace Messages In Matching Schema
description_text: Background Add OrderMassCancelRequest [q] and OrderMassCanceReport [r] messages to matching inbound FIX schema. Issues The messages, with corresponding fields and enums were added to the matching inbound FIX schema but were accidently removed in subsequent changes done for  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/CODE-5240 . We need to add these back to the schema. Changes Add back the OrderMassCancelRequest [q] and OrderMassCanceReport [r] messages with corresponding fields and enums to the matching inbound FIX schema. Flags We are adding messages to schema so it is non breaking, and areas is code.

summary: Order Mass Cancel Request/Report Integration With The FIX Gateway
description_text: Background In   and   we have created writers for the sending core commands from order mass cancel request and in [CODE-5305] we have created the event to FIX adapter. Issues The writers created in the previously mentioned were not integrated into a FIX gateway. Changes We create a single writers with a fallback writer to process the incoming order mass cancel FIX message. The reject writer will be invoked if the primary writer fails. This is integrated into the FixToCommand handler. We added the order mass cancel request and reject adapters to the event to FIX handler. Command file changes to configure and glue the various parts. End to end beaker tests. Flags The is new non-breaking feature hence feat/code.

summary: General FIX schema for Matching
description_text: Background A new matching product is introduced. This products requires an inbound FIX gateway to allow market participants to trade automatically. Issues To first step of building a FIX gateway is to create a FIX schema that contains all the tags that are relevant to the interface. This schema is used by the gateway to create a statically typed gateway which support a set of well known tags and only those tags. Changes Creating the schema is equivalent to creating a XML schema that is the difference between the business specific spec and generic FIX spec. For example, additional constraints that the domain specific spec introduces, or new custom tags altogether. To do so we will need to create three tables and one XML file.  For OrderSingle[D], CancelRequest[G], and CancelReplace[F] we should create a table in the ticket. Each table that defined the smallest most constraint difference from FIX50SP2 and the table's message. For example, if the TimInForce[59] tag can take only Day[0], we will have an entry in our table making that clear. The following is an example for some entries in a table. If "{*}" is indicated, it means that differs from FIX50SP2 i.e. if Y * is specified under Required column, it means its Optional in FIX50SP2. If none of the columns is indicated with a "{*}", it means its a custom tag. OrderSingle[D] Message Tag Name Type Possible values Max length Notes Required 11 ClOrdId text 32 * Y 21 HandlInst int 1 (Automated) * Y * 22 SecurityIDSource int 1(CUSIP), 4(ISIN) * Y * 38 OrderQty qty >= Bond's Min Increment Sz,  <= 9,999,000 Y * 40 OrdType char P(Pegged) * Y 48 SecurityID text Y * 54 Side char 1(Buy), 2(Sell) * Y 55 Symbol text "N/A" * Y * 59 TimeInForce char 0(Day) * Y * 423 PriceType int 1=%, 6=Spread, 9=Yield * Y * 447 PartyIDSource char D(Proprietary code) * Y * 448 PartyID text Y * 452 PartyRole int 1(Executing Trader) * Y * 453 NoPartyIDs int Y * 5528 SalesBook text Conditional - Dealer Order rejected if not valid C 20120 MKTXWorkflowTyp…

summary: FIX Mass Cancel Reports For Mass Cancel Request Accept And Reject Events
description_text: Background Nucleus supports an inbound FIX order gateway. Issues Nucleus Inbound FIX order gateway does not support Mass Cancel Request and report which are non-order specific messages. For the matching project we need to support these messages. We already have mass cancel report and mass cancel reject events. Both events result with a FIX OrderMassCancelReport[r] being sent to the client. This ticket is about translating the mass cancel request and mass cancel reject events to a mass cancel report to a client. We are going to use the object to FIX msg Adapter to build the outgoing FIX message. Changes We will create two new classes that extend ObjectToFixMsgAdapter to build the FIX OrderMassCancelReport[r] message. OrderMassCancelRequestToFixMsgAdapter for mass cancel request event, and OrderMassCancelRequestToFixMsgAdapter for mass cancel reject event. Flags This is a new non breaking feature, hence it is feat/code.

summary: Setup the Matching FIX Gateway to support Mass Cancel
description_text: Background In matching we need to support incoming OrderMassCancelRequest[q] and outgoing OrderMassCancelReport[r] in the inbound FIX order gateway. Issues We need to introduced these messages at app level and add basic dispatching and wiring logic in the inbound order gateway. Changes Create OrderMassCancelRequest event in app Create OrderMassCancelReject event in app Add method to handle OrderMassCancelRequest[q] to FixToCommandHandler Add methods to handle OrderMassCancelRequest and OrderMassCancelReject to EventToFixHandler In inbound fix order gateways connect event/fixMsg dispatching to these new methods Flags Not external visibility so Areas is code, and non-breaking

summary: Trading Fix Messages Schema
description_text: Background Nucleus allows to customize FIX schemas to a dialect which in turn can be used to build FIX gateway. Issues To build a FIX gateway for a specific dialect, Nucleus requires a "strongly" typed schema. That is a schema that defines the dialect. Any field constraints, e.g., length constraints, enums relationships, etc are expressed in the schema. In order to support the trading messages, we need to review and amend the current FIX schema for trading messages. Changes We should review the FIX spec in  https://marketaxesscorp.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/EWPxBrUcslNOnKapRxu9Y_MBHF39D6PYi0jNGF4Eznl-MQ?e=HBSeA6 . In a comment in the ticket we should document all the necessary changes relative to the current schema found in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml . In particular, 1. Any updates to FIX enums defined in the Nucleus schema,  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml 2. Any new custom FIX enums that we need to handle to support these messages. 3. Any standard FIX enums that we support only subset of the values? What are these values. We should make the following changes In the Nucleus schema fields section 1. For any existing FIX enum that we need to update, we should update both the values in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml  and in  matching-messages.xml . 2. For any new custom FIX enum we should add them as fields in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml  and new corresponding enums in the  matching-messages.xml . We should connect the two in the  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml . 3. For every standard FIX enum that we use a subset of the values, we should create a FIX enum in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml  that handle only that subset of values. In the Nucleus schema in the messages section 4. For any field that we are going to constrain, say by length, add a field in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml 's field section. 5. Create any missing messages in  matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml  that we will customize.  6. In each of the messages add the fields we are customiz…

summary: Speed up loading of Fix50 class
description_text: Background Fix50.java class that is generated from quickfix50.xml is too large and its loading takes about 15 seconds. Issues Slow loading of Fix50 caused multi-JVM integration tests for Matching on master branch to fail. Bringing up the inbound FIX gateway takes a long time and telnet listeners times out because of that. To mitigate this issue we had to force multi-JVM integration test for Matching to run in single JVM using in-proc network, however this is a temporary measure until we find a permanent solution. Changes To avoid the generated Fix50.java class from being too large, we need to create another version of quickfix50.xml that contains only messages and tags relevant to our Matching schema, and use it as a basis for MatchingInboundFix50 schema class. We will keep the original quickfix50.xml for reference only but it will not be used for class generation. We will add messages and fields to the new version of quickfix50.xml if that becomes necessary in the future. Flags This is a non-breaking change since no modules other than Matching are using this schema. Areas is code.

summary: Create a Matching Inbound FIX Order Gateway Shell
description_text: Background Creating an empty inbound FIX order gateway for matching Issues There is no inbound gateway for matching currently, we need to create one so we can use it in Beaker tests. Changes Create a matching inbound FIX order gateway and getting the Beaker test for session behavior passing. Create MatchingOrder/FixMatchingOrder and corresponding order service to use in InMemoryInboundClOrdIdService Create a commands file for Matching Inbound Order Gateway and configure Matching Beaker to use it to bring up the gateway Create a basic MatchingFixToCommandHandler for processing inbound matching orders Create a basic MatchingEventToFixHandler for processing outbound ER messages Flags Non-breaking change and areas is code.

summary: Matching Inbound Order Requests Handling - Part 1
description_text: Background To support the matching business we need to supported an inbound FIX order gateway for supporting automated interaction with the auction. With the schema done, we can progress to building the gateway itself. Issues In order to create a gateway we need to be able to process and incoming a FIX message and write a command to the core. The gateway process three business level messages, order requests, replace requests, and cancel requests. In this ticket we are going to work on the order requests. Changes We need to create a message writer that we will use in the gateway to process incoming OrderSingle[D] FIX messages. The message writer should follow the FIX spec to handle the various checks and validation. The FIX spec is in https://marketaxess.atlassian.net.rproxy.goskope.com/wiki/spaces/Pragma/pages/3465216953/FIX+Inbound+-+Mid-X Note: Validation on OrderQty for: >= Bond's Min Increment Sz and <= 9,999,000 to be handled in a separate ticket. Flags The is a new feature with no external impact and should be classified as  code

summary: Client Order Reject
description_text: Background Nucleus supports an FIX order request to command in the matching area Issues In order to complete the matching FIX inbound order gateway, we need to be able to reject an order request due to a malformed FIX order request. The order request FIX to command writer will end with a failure if the FIX message fails validation. When the validation fails we need to send a  ClientOrderReject  command to the core. This command needs to be generated of the FIX order request. The Nucleus framework handles all the linking and as a business developer all one needs to do is write a similar writer from order request to  ClientOrderReject Changes Analysis We should create the following table for all the tags that are required to be present on an order request reject FIX message. (Note that order request reject is an MsgType[35]=ExecReport[8] and not a CancelReject[9]. Tag Can be accommodated on the  ClientOrderRequestReject  [Y/N] Will be in Luggage Command Field Name ClOrdID (11) Y N ClOrdID NoPartyIDs (453) Y Y FixSupplemental PartyID (448) Y Y FixSupplemental PartyIDSource (447) Y Y FixSupplemental PartyRole (452) Y Y FixSupplemental TimeInForce (59) Y N TimeInForce Side (54) Y N Side PriceType (423) Y Y FixSupplemental Price (44) Y Y FixSupplemental Currency (15) Y Y FixSupplemental BenchmarkSecurityID (699) Y Y FixSupplemental BenchmarkSecurityIDSource (761) Y Y FixSupplemental MKTXWorkflowType (20120) Y Y FixSupplemental MatchingClientOrderRequestReject We should add to  matching-message.xml  and extension of the  ClientOrderRequestReject  with any additional required tag. Note, we should not edit the  matching-message.xml  before we review the table and the planned changes. Writer We will create the writer for sending the  MatchingClientOrderRequestReject  command Flags This is a new feature and it does not break any existing functionality.

summary: Mass Cancel Request Fix To Command: Part II Reject
description_text: Background The matching inbound order gateway supports a mass cancel request event. Issues A FIX mass cancel request can be either accepted or rejected. Our work in CODE-5221 does not capture the reject case. Changes Analysis We should create the following table Tag Should be in luggage[Y/N] Field Name Command Type 1369 N RejectId long 58 N RejectReasonText string 11 Y 447 Y 448 Y 452 Y 453 Y 530 Y 20120 Y Creating a command We Should create a new command  MatchingMassCancelRequestReject  based on the table. We should put here the message definition before we do it. <nbmext:message name="OrderMassCancelReject" id="36"  commandInterfaces="com.pragmafs.messages.fix.FixGatewayCommand" eventInterfaces="com.pragmafs.messages.fix.FixGatewayEvent" description="the rejection of order mass cancel request">         <required name="RejectId" type="long" spec="id" description="a unique identifier for order mass cancel reject"/>         <required name="RejectReasonText" type="string" description="the reason the order mass cancel request was rejected"/>         <optional name="FixSupplemental" id="1" type="FixSupplemental" description="FIX protocol additional information"/>         <optional name="Luggage" id="2" type="string_map" description="the pass-through fields related to the order mass cancel request"/>     </nbmext:message> Create a writer We will create a writer for sending the reject. ToDo Setting the reject reason text in order mass cancel reject command will be done in Setting PartyIDSource[447], PartyID[448], and PartyRole[452] tags in the reject luggage handler from the Parties repeating block will be done after Flags This is a new non-breaking feature and hence it is feat/code

summary: Mass Cancel FIX Schema
description_text: Background Nucleus allows to customize FIX schemas to a dialect which in turn can be used to build FIX gateway. Issues To build a FIX gateway for a specific dialect, Nucleus requires a "strongly" typed schema. That is a schema that defines the dialect. Any field constraints, e.g., length constraints, enums relationships, etc. For the MidX project we need to support Mass Cancel Request and Mass Cancel Response messages. In order to support these messages we need to, first, add to the Matching FIX schema the relevant changes for these messages. Changes We should inspect the FIX spec in  https://marketaxesscorp.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/Edv_lwBbWrFIsyVswUEp3hABPUgl6HfCHHK4_GueNayKpw?e=8peM1X  for both the MassCancelRequest[q] and MassCancelResponse[r] messages. In a comment in the ticket we should document all the necessary changes relative to the current schema found in matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml. In particular, 1. Any updates to FIX enums defined in the Nucleus schema, matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml 2. Any new custom FIX enums that we need to handle to support these messages. 3. Any standard FIX enums that we support only subset of the values? What are these values. We should make the following changes In the Nucleus schema fields section 1. For any existing FIX enum that we need to update, we should update both the values in matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml and in matching-messages.xml 2. For any new custom FIX enum we should add them as fields in matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml and new corresponding enums in the matching-messages.xml. We should connect the two in the matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml. 3. For every standard FIX enum that we use a subset of the values, we should create a FIX enum in matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml that handle only that subset of values. In the Nucleus schema in the messages section 4. Create two new messages in matching-inbound-quickfix-50.xml for MassCancelRequest and MassCancelResponse. Use  code <message name="MassCancelReques…

summary: Add Header To The QuickFix 5.0 Dictionary
description_text: Background FIX messages have standard header that all the messages inherit. Every message should have one. Issues The Nucleus 5.0 dictionary, which can be found in quickfix50.xml does not contain the header in that spec. We should add the header to the dictionary. The FIX 5.0 spec contains components in the header. As part of this we might need to change the generation to account for components in the generate code's header. Changes REQUIRED – Describe what changes are necessary to address the issues in the previous section. Flags This has no external impact and hence it is code

summary: Inbound Order Request Fix To Command Writer
description_text: Background The Nucleus system has multiple inbound FIX order Gateways. Issues The Matching system requires an FIX order gateway based on the Matching FIX spec for MidX project. Changes Analysis We should create the following table for all the tags that are required to be present on an order request FIX message. Tag Can be accommodated on the  OrderRequest  [Y/N] Will be in Luggage Command Field Name ClOrdID (11) Y N ClOrdID NoPartyIDs (453) N Y FixSupplemental PartyID (448) N Y FixSupplemental PartyIDSource (447) N Y FixSupplemental PartyRole (452) N Y FixSupplemental HandlInst (21) Y N HandlInst Symbol (55) N N Fixed value "NA" SecurityID (48) Y N InstrumentId SecurityIDSource (22) Y N SecuritySource Side (54) Y N Side OrderQty (38) Y N Qty MKTXOrderType (29776) Y N MktxLmOrderType PriceType (423) Y N PriceType OrdType (40) Y N OrderType BenchmarkSecurityID (699) Y N BenchmarkInstrumentId BenchmarkSecurityIDSource (761) Y N BenchmarkSecuritySource MKTXWorkflowType (20120) Y N VenueId To implement FIX order gateway that supports MidX Matching FIX spec, we need the followings:- Create MatchingFixToOrderRequestCommandWriter Create MatchingFixToOrderRequestCommandWriterTest Create MatchingInboundFixLuggageHandler Create MatchingInboundFixLuggageHandlerTest Update MatchingCachingServiceFactory to support holding company lookup in test Flags Area is external because this updates the FIX gateway. Non-breaking change because this is a new feature and currently not in production. Hence feat/code.

summary: Mass Cancel Request Fix To Command: Part I No Reject
description_text: Background Nucleus does not handle mass cancel request FIX messages. Issues The matching system requires to handle the OrderMassCancelRequest(q) message. An order mass cancel request FIX message can be rejected or accepted. In this ticket we are going to handle only the acceptance case, i.e., we will not handle error but report them. The goal of this ticket is to create a MatchingFixToOrderMassCancelRequestCommandWriter. See  BookTradingOrderRequestMessageWriter  for example. We will use the OrderMassCancelRequestCommand. Changes We will approach it in two main steps. Create a class Test the class Create a class In this part we will implement the writer. In the writer we will have three parts 1. FIX level validation - You should validate the existence of the required tags 2. Writing the command - You should write the tags and the luggage. The four party tags should be luggage. 3. Validating the command - Validating the conditional tag. 4. In your constructor you will need UserService Things to add. Assuming you did the schema right, you should be OK. <nbmext:refType name="ClOrdId" type="string" encodedLength="32" description="TBD"/> /// <nbmext:message name="MassCancel" id="X" description="X"> <required name="ClOrdId" type="HoldingCompanyId" spec="id" description="a unique identifier of the holding company"/> <required name="TraderId" type="UserId" description="the id of a trader sending this order request"/> <required name="MassCancelRequestType" type="MassCancelRequestType" description="Please define the enum"/> <required name="MktxWorkflowType" type="MktxWorkflowType" description="TBD"/> </nbmext:message> Note ClOrdIdNotInSessionValidator  in the platform can be used to validate the ClOrderId. Order 1. Set the messages as above. 2. You will need to copy from book trading  CompanyTraderIdCommandWriter  with its tests but remove the company portion from it.  3. Create an empty FixToCommandWriter (with the right name) and write the Javadoc. 4. Populate all the tags.…

summary: Set Reject Reason Text In Order Mass Cancel Reject Command Writer
description_text: Background Order mass cancel reject command writer creates a command when an order mass cancel request is rejected to send a FIX OrderMassCancelReport [r] message back to the client. Issues The order mass cancel report message provides a reject reason in Text[58] tag. This value is not available in the reject command writer. Changes Provide an appropriate reject reason text to MatchingFixToOrderMassCancelRejectCommandWriter which then sets it on the reject command. The reject reason text is a required field in the command. Flags This is new functionality so it's non breaking, and areas is code.

summary: Auctions Inbound FIX Order Gateway
description_text: Summary Create an inbound FIX order gateway for Nucleus based Auctions orders. The FIX spec is here:  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Pragma/pages/3465216953/FIX+Inbound+-+Mid-X Goals Create a production grade inbound FIX order gateway for handling inbound FIX Auctions orders only. Non-Goals It is not a goal to implement parts of the FIX spec that are not Auctions related. Certify the gateway externally. Implement any business level messages other than OrderSingle[D], ReplaceRequest[F], CancelRequest[G], MassCancelRequest[q]. Motivation A new Auctions product is going to be offered by MarketAxess. Auction orders will be supported by both UI based and FIX based workflows. This will allow for both manual traders automated traders to participate in the auction, covering the full spectrum of market participants. FIX is the most popular standards for sending orders to another system, and implementing an Auctions FIX spec will allow the automated traders to participate in the auction session. Nucleus based implementation of Auctions will implement the inbound FIX orders gateway interface for orders. This implementation will grow over time with the introduction of more products to the Nucleus ecosystem. However, this epic is narrowly minded to Auctions in order to limit the scope and guarantee success. Description FIX version Auctions will use FIX 5.0SP2. A new schema should be created to handle the Auctions specific tags, limitations, and core <-> relationships. Orders Order definition in support for Auctions should be created as it is going to be used for FIX drops, feed etc. Fix to command The gateway should handle FIX to commands for OrderSingle[D], ReplaceRequest[G], CancelRequest[F] and MassCancelRequest[q]. This should be done via the FixToCommand handler. Event to FIX Using the OrderToFixAdaptor we should construct an EventToFix handler for generating FIX messages from events. Gateway A new FIX gateway should be created. The gateway should include the…

summary: Integrate custom pegged order functionality with X-Pro
description_text: Ensure that the new custom pegged order features are fully integrated with the X-Pro platform, including any necessary API, UI, or backend changes.

summary: Update matching engine to support custom pegged order priority rules
description_text: Background Pegged orders are prioritized before limit orders in the matching engine, due to the assumption that their limit price would be more broadly executable (more aggressive) than a limit order’s limit. Issues With the new pegged order dynamic limit, there may be scenarios where the limits or limit orders and the dynamic limit of pegged orders would intersect. Changes Update the matching engine prioritization logic to include the price of the orders, with more aggressive prices getting priority. Flags This is a breaking change as the functionality of the matching engine is changing. Areas is code and external.

summary: Add validations for custom pegged orders
description_text: Background Auction supports pegged orders which are configured via two fields: pegOffset and pegType. Issues While the fields are available on the request objects, they are not validated. Changes Validate the pegOffset and pegType fields with respect to the order request on submission. Both fields should be required for a pegged order, and both fields should not be populated if the order is not pegged. Also validate that the calculated dynamic limit from the pegged order, would be within the static guardrails the session. Flags Non-breaking as this is new functionality. Areas is code.

summary: Implement limit price calculation logic for custom pegged orders
description_text: Background Custom pegged orders are pegged to the CP+ value indicated by the pegType (Mid or Aggressive) +/- the value indicated by pegOffset. Issues We need to calculate the limit price for a custom pegged order based on the peg type and peg offset. This value will be sent on AuctionsMatchRequestCommand in the price field, to be used for matching later. Changes Modify AuctionsMatchRequestPriceFieldWriter to account for pegType and pegOffset on the order according to the following rules: if order’s pegType is MIDPOINT, the calculated price would be CP+ Mid +/- order’s pegOffset. If the offset is negative, that means less aggressive, if positive then it’s more aggressive. So for price buy orders and sell spread/yield/dm orders, add the offset to the CP+ Mid. For all others, subtract the offset from the CP+ Mid. if order’s pegType is MARKET (aggressive), the calculated price is CP+ on the opposite side +/- order’s pegOffset. Same rules as above apply for adding/subtracting peg offset. if order’s orderType is pegged but it has no pegType or pegOffset, apply the same calculation that exists right now. Flags This is a new functionality so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Update commands and mutators to support custom pegged orders
description_text: Background Matching supports different order types, with pegged orders having limits which are “pegged” to the CP+ value of the order’s side. Issues The pegged orders should additionally support a more complex “dynamic peg” option, which is defined  here. Changes Update the FlexOrderRequest, OrderRequest, RtsFlexOrderRequest, and FlexOrderReplaceRequest commands to include the two new fields in the external api: pegType (an enum of Mid or Agg) pegOffset (a bps value indicating the offset from either the CP+ mid or agg depending on the pegType) pendingPegType pendingPegOffset Additionally, update the auctions OLU drop with these new fields. Flags The changes introduce two new fields and change the external api, but are still non-breaking due to the fields being optional. If an existing client does nothing to change their interaction with the api, nothing will break. Areas is code, external.

summary: Update API documentation for custom pegged orders
description_text: Background Custom Pegged orders is a new type of orders that have 2 additional fields: peg type and peg offset. Peg Type is the reference value to peg to (Mid or Aggressive), and peg offset is the offset from the reference level. Issues We need to make changes to the Auctions orders API to include these 2 additional fields on the order requests and OLU. These new fields are optional. Changes Update flex_new_order_request, new_order_request, replace_order_request and order_lifecycle_update with these fields Update auctions_orders_actions_api document describing constraints for these fields, i.e. peg offset should only be present is peg type is present, etc. Do the same for RTS APIs Flags This is a documentation only ticket, so it’s non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Acceptance tests for custom pegged orders in Beaker
description_text: Background Pegged orders have their own validations in the matching UI feed and prioritization logic in the matching engine, based on a calculated “pegged limit”. Issues There are no acceptance tests in place to verify that these features work as expected. Changes Create acceptance tests to verify that pegged orders are handled as expected in both the feed and matching engine. Flags Non-breaking as this change amounts to adding tests. Areas is code.

summary: Auctions Custom Pegged Orders
description_text: Summary Currently in Auctions, users can specify their order type as Pegged, which means that they are pegging their limit price to the CP+ Mid. These orders have a higher priority when matching than limit orders. The requirement for this epic is to implement custom pegged orders, where users can specify an offset from a CP+ value (mid or aggressive) to peg their limit price to. Goals To implement the above feature, described in detail here: Non-Goals It’s a non-goal to implement more complex CP+ custom peg functionality, such as pegging by percentage Success Metrics Success of this epic would mean that the CP+ custom peg functionality has been implemented, tested in Beaker acceptance tests, integrated with X-Pro and successfully verified in QA. Motivation This is an ask from the PO based on the Auction clients' requests. Description The following is needed to implement this functionality: Changes to the API documentation to add new fields to the order request/replace and OLU messages (peg offset (could be positive or negative), peg type (Mid/Agg)) Adding these fields to commands/mutators Calculation of the limit price based on the custom peg data Validations: Invalid combination of order type, peg type and offset Check the calculated limit price against CP+ guardrails Priority changes in Matching engine to account for new rules for custom pegged order
